The '''Dúnedain''' (Sindarin: "Men of the West") are the descendants of the Númenóreans, the great race of Men blessed with long life who founded the kingdoms of [[Gondor]] and Arnor in [[Middle-earth]]. After the destruction of the island kingdom of Númenor in the [[Middle-earth|Second Age]], the faithful Dúnedain established realms-in-exile under Elendil and his sons Isildur and Anárion.

By the time of the [[War of the Ring]], the northern Dúnedain had dwindled to a small band of Rangers who guarded [[The Shire]] and the north in secret. Their chieftain was [[Aragorn]], the last heir of Isildur, who restored the line of kings after the defeat of [[Sauron]].

== Notable Dúnedain ==
* [[Aragorn]] — the last Chieftain, who became King Elessar
* [[Boromir]] — son of the Steward of [[Gondor]]
* [[Faramir]] — brother of Boromir, Prince of Ithilien
* Isildur — who cut the [[One Ring]] from [[Sauron]]'s hand
* Elendil — the first High King of the Dúnedain in [[Middle-earth]]

[[Category:Dúnedain]]
